title: asperula oligantha var. scoparia
intro: Asperula oligantha var. scoparia, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and distinctive scent. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Asperula oligantha var. scoparia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic. Asperula oligantha var. scoparia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
